content:
⚖️ CPC (Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

📚 Most Important Orders & Rules Every Law Student and Advocate Must Know

If you're preparing for the Judiciary, APO, PCS-J, UGC-NET (Law), or practicing civil litigation, these are the most important provisions of the CPC:

📌 Order I – Parties to Suit
📌 Order VI – Pleadings
📌 Order VII Rule 11 – Rejection of Plaint ⭐
📌 Order VIII Rule 1 – Written Statement
📌 Order IX Rule 13 – Setting Aside Ex Parte Decree ⭐
📌 Order XIV – Framing of Issues
📌 Order XVIII – Hearing of the Suit & Evidence
📌 Order XX – Judgment & Decree
📌 Order XXI – Execution of Decree ⭐ (Most Important)
📌 Order XXIII – Withdrawal & Compromise of Suit
📌 Order XXXIX Rules 1 & 2 – Temporary Injunction ⭐
📌 Order XLI Rule 5 – Stay of Decree in Appeal

💡 Remember:
"No Civil Litigation Practice is Complete Without a Strong Understanding of CPC.

comment: In practice, most case outcomes are shaped far earlier by O7 R11 objections and O39 injunction battles, while O21 execution remains the real bottleneck post-decree
